name: xve-vscode
description: Visual editor for hand-written XAML files — structure tree, live preview and surgical save, inside VS Code.

commands.title: XVE: Open in XAML Visual Editor

xve.language.description: UI language for the XAML Visual Editor. Empty = follow VS Code.

enumDescriptions: Ask once per project which DLLs / styles / dictionaries to load (WPF host)
Load all detected project resources automatically
Do not load project resources (built-in types only)
xve.project.autoLoadResources.markdownDescription: WPF host: how to load project resources (custom-control DLLs, `App.xaml`, theme/resource dictionaries) for faithful preview of custom types. The choice is remembered per project.

xve.sync.selectInTextEditor.description: When you select an element in the visual editor, move the cursor to it in a side-by-side text editor.

xve.sync.selectFromTextCursor.description: When the cursor moves in a side-by-side text editor, select the matching element in the visual editor.

xve.editor.highlightChanges.description: Color changed lines in the text editor (mirrors the "Show changes in code" toggle in the Changes panel).

xve.editor.highlightErrors.description: Color and underline the line of a preview/render error in the text editor (mirrors the "Show errors in code" toggle in the WPF host log).

enumDescriptions: Paste exactly as copied. Pasting an element that has an x:Name/Name already present in the document creates a duplicate name (a build error in the project).
Rename x:Name/Name values that collide with existing names in the target document to a unique variant (e.g. btnOk_Copy). The source element is never modified.
Like "rename", and also update internal references (Binding ElementName, x:Reference) within the pasted subtree so the copy stays self-consistent.
xve.paste.nameDeduplication.description: How to handle x:Name/Name collisions when pasting an element into the visual editor.

enumDescriptions: Auto-detect (WPF host on Windows, web renderer elsewhere)
Ask whether to isolate when a file is from another project or has no project
Auto: isolate when needed (file from another project / no project) without asking; share within the open project
Never isolate: always share a single WPF host and its resources across all windows
Always isolate: run a separate WPF host with its own resources for each file
xve.preview.isolation.markdownDescription:
WPF host isolation policy. **Isolated** mode runs a fresh WPF host process with its own loaded resources for a file, so resources loaded by other windows/projects don't change how it renders.

- `ask` — for a file from another project (or with no project) ask whether to isolate it.
- `auto` — isolate such files automatically, without asking; share one host within the open project/workspace.
- `shared` — never isolate (one host for everything).
- `isolated` — always isolate (a separate host per file).

The choice can be overridden per window from the preview toolbar/engine selector.

xve.preview.renderScale.markdownDescription: WPF host render scale (supersampling). `auto` renders at the display's device-pixel resolution for a crisp preview on HiDPI screens. Higher values increase sharpness and CPU/memory use.

xve.preview.maxResolution.markdownDescription: Maximum WPF host bitmap size (longer side, in **device** pixels). Caps memory/transfer for large surfaces or high render scale. `0` = unlimited (full sharpness).

enumDescriptions: Native web look (GTK/Linux) — web renderer only
xve.preview.theme.description: Preview theme. Fluent/classic mirror the WPF host (via ThemeMode); 'native' is a GTK/Linux-style look for the web renderer (the WPF host falls back to classic).

xve.preview.viewportRender.description: WPF host: render only the visible region (viewport) instead of the whole surface. Faster for large designs.

enumDescriptions: Apply the resolution cap to the visible area only — the same on-screen fragment stays equally sharp regardless of window size (overscan keeps the same density).
Apply the resolution cap to the whole rendered slice including overscan (legacy behavior).
xve.preview.capBasis.markdownDescription: WPF host (visible-area render): what the `maxResolution` cap is measured against.

xve.preview.overscan.description: WPF host (visible-area render): extra margin (in project units) rendered around the visible region as a buffer for scrolling.

xve.preview.debugConsole.description: WPF host: show a debug console at the bottom of the preview with live render telemetry (render size, frame time, scale, viewbox, …).

xve.preview.consoleOnStart.description: WPF host: dock the console at the bottom of the preview while the host is starting up. When off, the console stays hidden during start-up but still appears automatically on a render/preview error.

xve.preview.debugLiveDrag.description: Debug console: also update render telemetry on every frame while dragging an element in the preview.

xve.preview.dragStrategy.description: WPF host live-preview strategy while dragging/resizing.

xve.preview.dragIntervalMs.description: Drag strategy 'ms': minimum interval between live re-renders (milliseconds).

xve.preview.dragFrames.description: Drag strategy 'frames': re-render every N animation frames.

xve.preview.dragCoalesce.description: WPF host: keep at most one render in flight during drag/scroll (drop stale frames).

xve.preview.dragSession.description: WPF host: use a persistent drag session (parse once, mutate cached tree) instead of a full re-render each frame.

xve.preview.dragOnChange.description: WPF host: during a drag, render a new frame only when the dragged element's attributes actually change (skip repeat frames when the pointer is held still).

xve.preview.adaptiveRes.markdownDescription: WPF host: adaptive resolution — render at a low resolution (`motionResolution`) while dragging or scrolling/zooming, then re-render once at full `maxResolution` after motion stops. Smoother fast motion, sharp when idle.

xve.preview.motionResolution.description: WPF host: render resolution (longer side, device px) used while moving when adaptive resolution is on.

xve.preview.adaptiveFpsThreshold.markdownDescription: WPF host: only drop to the motion resolution while moving when the full-resolution render runs below this many FPS. `0` = always use the motion resolution while moving.

xve.preview.fitOnOpen.description: Fit the preview to the window when a document opens: shrink to fit if it is larger than the view, otherwise show at 100% (never magnify). The per-window state is remembered.

xve.canvas.gridStep.description: Step & snap distance in pixels: grid step (when the grid is shown) and the snap threshold to guides (when rulers are shown).

xve.canvas.showGrid.description: Default for showing the dot grid overlay in the preview (toggled live from the toolbar).

xve.canvas.showRulers.description: Default for showing rulers (and guides) in the preview (toggled live from the toolbar).

scripts.build:host:dist: dotnet publish wpf-host -c Release -r win-x64 --self-contained false -o wpf-host/dist/win-x64 && dotnet publish wpf-host -c Release -r win-arm64 --self-contained false -o wpf-host/dist/win-arm64
